reasoning: The inner `⋃ (a : ℚ) (b : ℚ) (_ : a < b), {Set.Ioo (a : ℝ) b}` produces precisely the collection of open intervals $(a,b) \subset \mathbb{R}$ with rational endpoints $a<b$. Applying `Set.image2 (fun s t => s ×ˢ t)` to two such collections yields exactly $\{(a,b) \times (c,d) \mid a,b,c,d \in \mathbb{Q}, a<b, c<d\}$. Since `ℝ × ℝ` carries the product topology (which agrees with the standard Euclidean topology on $\mathbb{R}^2$), `IsTopologicalBasis` on this collection states exactly that it is a basis for the topology of $\mathbb{R}^2$. No hypotheses or conclusions are altered, so this is a faithful formalization.
